blocks.heading: Turn a git target into a self-contained HTML review — and own the **review shell** every other product page reuses.

rows.value: 19 — one CLI entry, eight pipeline modules, ten review-shell renderers.

The **visual-recap** product: resolve a PR / commit / branch / working-tree into a diff, parse and rank it, fold it into a `Block[]`, and render the standing review document. The shell it renders is shared infrastructure — **atlas** and **authoring** assemble their pages on top of the same topbar / sidebar / sections vocabulary (shared CSS + viewer + section helpers).

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "recap is a pipeline, and its renderer is everyone's renderer."
bigIdea.sub: `Target → git.ts → parse-diff → diff-order → gather-recap → assemble-review → recap.html`. The gather half is mechanical and TS/JS-aware; the render half (`assemble-review` + `src/review/*`) is the **app-shell** — topbar, sidebar, TL;DR, files-table, walkthrough, diagrams — whose CSS, viewer script, and section helpers `assemble-atlas` and `assemble-spec` reuse to draw *their* pages. Enrich the gathered `blocks.json`, re-render with `recap --blocks`, and nothing in the pipeline needs to change.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: A thin CLI drives a mechanical gather pipeline (git target → diff → ranked `Block[]`) into a shared render shell. Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: CLI
cards.purpose: The entry point: parse `--pr` / `--commit` / `--branch` (default: the working tree) into a `Target`, gather blocks, and write `recap.html` + `blocks.json`. The `--blocks` path re-renders an enriched `blocks.json` straight through the shell, skipping git.

cards.name: git targets
cards.purpose: Resolve a `Target` into a `Scope` — base/head refs, label, and the raw unified diff — via `git` (and `gh` for PRs). Also reads file contents at a ref and a structured changed-files list.

cards.name: diff parsing
cards.purpose: Pure string work: `parseUnifiedDiff` turns the raw diff into one `DiffBlock` (with hunks) per file; `sortByImportance` ranks files so source leads and lockfiles trail.

cards.name: gather
cards.purpose: The orchestrator. `gatherRecap` runs the whole pipeline; `buildBlocks` composes the ordered `Block[]` — summary, file-tree, where-it-fits, schema/api diagrams, then the ranked diffs. `summaryMarkdown` writes the mechanical lead.

cards.name: review shell
cards.purpose: The app-shell — `assembleReview` plus the `src/review/*` renderers (topbar, sidebar, TL;DR, files-table, sections, walkthrough, diagrams, normalize). The shared HTML surface every product page is built on.

cards.name: api-diagram
cards.purpose: Builds an architecture diagram of the changed API surface (routers → procedures, colored by add/change/remove) — d2 floor plus an editable mermaid upgrade.

cards.name: completeness lint
cards.purpose: The diff-size-aware "demo standard" lint: warns (never blocks) when a code recap lacks a lead overview, leaves non-trivial diffs unannotated, or piles ungrouped diffs into a flat list.

blocks.title: Internal architecture
blocks.intro: `gatherRecap` is the spine. A `Target` resolves through `git.ts` to a `Scope` (base/head + raw diff); `parse-diff` and `diff-order` turn the diff into ranked `DiffBlock`s; `buildBlocks` folds them — plus a summary, where-it-fits, and api-surface diagrams — into a `Block[]`; `assembleReview` renders that through the `src/review/*` shell to `recap.html`. The shell is a shared seam: `assemble-atlas` and `assemble-spec` draw *their* pages with its CSS, viewer script, and `sections.ts` helpers.
blocks.callout: The render half (`assemble-review` + `src/review/*`) is the **shared review shell**; `assemble-atlas` and `assemble-spec` draw their own pages with its CSS, viewer script, and `sections.ts` helpers.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece — what it does, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the domain.

components.name: CLI
detail: `bin/recap.ts` is the whole command-line surface. `parseArgs` reads `--pr` / `--commit` / `--branch` (`--base` to override), defaulting to the working tree when no flag is given, which `parseTarget` folds into a `Target` discriminated union. The normal path calls [gatherRecap](#c-gather) to get `{ scope, blocks, adapter }`, then [assembleReview](#c-shell) to produce `recap.html`. Output is written into a directory (a trailing `.html` is stripped): the HTML, any `.excalidraw` sidecars, and a sibling `blocks.json` all live together so the recap is self-contained.

The `--blocks` flag is the enrichment round-trip: it loads an already-gathered (and hand-enriched) `blocks.json` and renders it straight through [assembleReview](#c-shell), skipping git entirely. That separation — gather once, enrich the JSON, re-render — is what lets the visual-recap skill add overviews, annotations, and groupings without re-running the diff. `--emit-blocks` writes the raw `Block[]` without HTML, for the same loop.

files.name: bin/recap.ts
files.desc: Arg parsing, the gather→assemble path, and the `--blocks` re-render path.

exports.name: main()
exports.desc: The CLI entry; `main().catch` exits non-zero on error.

exports.name: parseTarget()
exports.desc: Flag values → a `Target` (`pr` / `commit` / `branch` / `working`).

components.name: git targets
detail: `resolveScope` is the only place that shells out. It maps each `Target` kind to a base/head pair: a commit diffs against its `^` parent, a branch against an explicit `--base` or the merge-base with `main`/`master` (`defaultBase`), the working tree against `HEAD`, and a PR is first `gh pr checkout`'d (the one hard dependency on the `gh` CLI) then treated as `HEAD` vs its merge-base. It returns a `Scope` — `repoRoot`, `baseRef`, `headRef`, a human `label`, and the raw `unifiedDiff`.

`changedFiles` is the structured companion: it merges `git diff --numstat -z` and `--name-status -z` into `FileChange` rows (path, A/M/D/R status, added/deleted counts). The NUL-delimited (`-z`) form is deliberate — it expands renames into explicit old/new tokens so a renamed file keeps its real new path and an `R` status. `fileAtRef` reads a file's contents at a ref (falling back to a working-tree read), used by the schema/api adapters downstream.

exports.name: resolveScope()
exports.desc: → `Scope` — base/head refs, label, and the raw unified diff.

exports.name: changedFiles()
exports.desc: → `FileChange[]` — path, status, +/- counts, rename-aware.

exports.name: fileAtRef()
exports.desc: File contents at a ref (or working tree), `""` if absent.

exports.name: Target
exports.desc: The git-target union (`pr` / `commit` / `branch` / `working`).

exports.name: Scope
exports.desc: The resolved scope: base/head refs, label, raw diff.

connections.body: `git` for diffs; `gh pr checkout` for PR targets

components.name: diff parsing
detail: `parseUnifiedDiff` is a small, pure state machine over the raw diff text: a `diff --git` line opens a new `DiffBlock` (title = basename, `path` = the `b/` path), an `@@` line opens a `DiffHunk`, and `+`/`-`/` ` lines append to the current hunk (the `+++`/`---` file headers are skipped). No git, no I/O — just string work, which keeps it trivially testable.

`sortByImportance` is a stable rank sort so the reader meets the substance first: source code ranks 0, schema/config 2, styles 3, tests 4, and lockfiles/generated files 5 — ties preserve input order. [buildBlocks](#c-gather) runs both together (`sortByImportance(parseUnifiedDiff(...))`) so the diffs land in the block array already ordered.

exports.name: parseUnifiedDiff()
exports.desc: Raw diff → one `DiffBlock` (with `DiffHunk`s) per file.

exports.name: sortByImportance()
exports.desc: Stable rank sort: source → config → styles → tests → generated.

components.name: gather
detail: `gatherRecap` is the top of the pipeline: resolve the [scope](#c-git), get the [changed files](#c-git), `selectAdapter` (currently the Prisma/tRPC adapter, else a no-op default), then hand off to `buildBlocks`. It returns `{ scope, blocks, adapter }` for the [CLI](#c-cli) to render.

`buildBlocks` composes the ordered `Block[]` that *is* the recap. It pushes a `file-tree`, asks the adapter for a schema diff and an API diff (each guarded so a failure only warns), unshifts a mechanical `summaryMarkdown` lead to the front, appends the code-intel `dependencyNeighborhood` "where it fits" diagram and the [api-surface](#c-api) diagram, then the [ranked diffs](#c-parse) last. `summaryMarkdown` writes that lead — file/line totals, areas touched, and added/removed/changed procedures — as pure Markdown from the already-gathered data. The result is raw material: the visual-recap skill enriches it (overviews, annotations, groups) before the final render.

exports.name: gatherRecap()
exports.desc: Target → `{ scope, blocks, adapter }` — the whole pipeline.

exports.name: buildBlocks()
exports.desc: Compose the ordered `Block[]`: summary, files, diagrams, diffs.

exports.name: summaryMarkdown()
exports.desc: The mechanical Markdown lead (totals, areas, procedure deltas).

components.name: review shell
detail: `assembleReview(blocks, opts)` is the app-shell: it asserts unique ids, normalizes loose top-level diffs into walkthrough chapters (`groupLooseDiffs`), inlines `review.css` + the viewer script + the dark-mode toggle assets (`theme-head.js` in `<head>`, `theme-toggle.js` after the viewer, `theme.css` appended last in `<style>`), renders every diagram once, then walks the blocks emitting `<section>`s — TL;DR + overview, files-changed, the guided walkthrough (progress rail + chapters), standalone diagram/schema/api/prose sections — into one self-contained HTML document. Along the way it runs `lintBlocks` and [lintCompleteness](#c-lint), surfacing warnings (including any diagram that failed to compile) through `onWarn`.

The `src/review/*` renderers are the shell's vocabulary, each a focused function: `topbar` (title + risk/stat chips), `sidebar` (files + walkthrough outline + meta + progress rail), `tldr` (the What/Why/Risk/Size card + overview points), `files-table`, `walkthrough` (chapters and per-file subsections with diff bodies), `diff` (the raw +/- body), `sections` (diagram cards, the rendered legend, api-surface cards, reused-block fallbacks), `diagrams` (collect + render all), and `normalize` (`stripChapterOrdinal`, `groupLooseDiffs`). **This shell is the domain's biggest shared seam** — `assemble-atlas` and `assemble-spec` build the atlas and spec pages on the same `review.css` + viewer script and the same topbar/sidebar/section class vocabulary (importing `sections.ts` helpers like `renderDiagramCard` / `withDiagramSvgClass` directly, while emitting their own topbar/sidebar HTML), so the chrome looks and behaves identically across all three products.

files.name: assemble-review.ts
files.desc: The orchestrator: normalize, render diagrams, emit sections, lint, write HTML.

files.name: review/topbar.ts
files.desc: The topbar chrome — title plus risk/stat chips.

files.name: review/sidebar.ts
files.desc: Files + walkthrough outline + meta; also the progress rail.

files.name: review/tldr.ts
files.desc: The TL;DR card + numbered overview points.

files.name: review/walkthrough.ts
files.desc: Chapters & per-file subsections with diff bodies.

files.name: review/diff.ts
files.desc: The raw +/- diff body with line numbers.

files.name: review/sections.ts
files.desc: Diagram/api/prose section cards + the rendered legend.

exports.name: assembleReview()
exports.desc: → the full self-contained review HTML; `ReviewOpts` / `ReviewStatus` shape its inputs.

exports.name: renderTopbar
exports.desc: The topbar chrome — title + risk/stat chips.

exports.name: renderSidebar
exports.desc: The nav — files, walkthrough outline, meta.

exports.name: renderTldr
exports.desc: The What/Why/Risk/Size TL;DR card.

exports.name: renderFilesTable
exports.desc: The files-changed table.

exports.name: renderWalkthrough
exports.desc: Chapters with per-file diff subsections.

exports.name: renderDiffBody
exports.desc: The raw +/- diff body.

exports.name: renderDiagramCard
exports.desc: Zoomable diagram card + legend.

exports.name: renderApiSurface
exports.desc: One card per changed procedure.

exports.name: groupLooseDiffs
exports.desc: Wrap loose top-level diffs into synthetic chapters.

exports.name: assertUniqueIds
exports.desc: Throw on any duplicate block id.

exports.name: collectDiagrams
exports.desc: Every diagram across the blocks, for one render pass.

connections.body: the `Block[]` from [gather](#c-gather) and the [core](../domain-core/domain-core.html) block types
`assemble-atlas` (atlas pages) and `assemble-spec` (authoring) — shared `review.css`/viewer plus the `sections.ts` helpers

components.name: api-diagram
detail: `apiSurfaceDiagram` turns a list of `ApiProcedure`s (gathered by the stack adapter) into an architecture diagram of the changed API surface. It groups procedures by router, emits a d2 floor (`client -> router`, each procedure a node colored by its `change` — added/changed/removed), and a parallel mermaid upgrade with `subgraph` routers and the shared `classDef`s so the figure is editable in Excalidraw. It returns `null` when there are no procedures, so [buildBlocks](#c-gather) simply skips the section.

exports.name: apiSurfaceDiagram()
exports.desc: → `DiagramBlock | null` — routers→procedures, colored by change.

connections.body: [the review shell](#c-shell) via the shared [diagrams](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html) renderer

components.name: completeness lint
detail: `lintCompleteness` is the diff-size-aware "demo standard" check — warnings only, it never throws or blocks rendering. If a recap has no diffs it is not a code recap and nothing is enforced. Otherwise it checks three things: a lead `overview` block exists with complete What/Why/Size facets and a risk level; every non-trivial diff (more than `TRIVIAL_DIFF_LINES` changed lines) carries a description; and once there are `GROUP_THRESHOLD`+ top-level diffs they are wrapped into `group` chapters rather than left as a flat pile. [assembleReview](#c-shell) runs it over the *original* blocks and routes its messages through `onWarn`, nudging the visual-recap skill to enrich the gather output past raw material.

exports.name: lintCompleteness()
exports.desc: → `string[]` of completeness warnings, in document order.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: recap owns the **review app-shell vocabulary** and the diff model; it borrows the generic `Block` union from core.

rows.name: Review app-shell
rows.desc: The standing page vocabulary — **topbar**, **sidebar**, **TL;DR**, **files-table**, **sections**, **walkthrough** (chapters + subsections + progress rail) — defined by `assemble-review.ts` + `src/review/*` and reused by atlas + authoring.

rows.name: FileChange
rows.desc: A changed file: `path`, `status` (A/M/D/R), and added/deleted line counts — rename-aware via `git diff -z`.

rows.name: DiffBlock · DiffHunk
rows.desc: The parsed diff model: one block per file (title, path, hunks), each hunk an `@@` header plus its `+`/`-`/` ` lines.

rows.name: Target · Scope
rows.desc: The git input union (`pr` / `commit` / `branch` / `working`) and the resolved scope (base/head refs, label, raw diff).

rows.name: ReviewOpts · ReviewStatus
rows.desc: The render options (title, source, status, outDir) and the topbar status pill (`green`/`yellow`/`red`).

blocks.note: Reads (never owns): the generic `Block` union, `OverviewBlock`, `ApiProcedure`, and `DiagramBlock` from `src/blocks.ts` ([core](../domain-core/domain-core.html)). The `FileChange` / `DiffBlock` / `DiffHunk` types are declared there too — recap owns their meaning as sole producer, not the declarations.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: A git target in; a self-contained review document out — and a render shell shared with every other product.

exposes.note: `assembleReview` + `src/review/*` — `assemble-atlas` and `assemble-spec` reuse its CSS/viewer and `sections.ts` helpers

blocks.note: It borrows the `Block` model + diagram rendering from `core`/`diagrams`, and the `dependencyNeighborhood` (where-it-fits) graph and api/schema diffs from `code-intel`. In return it **exposes the review shell** that atlas (`assemble-atlas`) and authoring (`assemble-spec`) build their pages on — the one seam where this surface domain is infrastructure for the rest.
